summary: Tested e-CF credential management feature (Settings > Sistema tab). All 10 backend API tests passed. Frontend UI verified: e-CF toggle, provider selector (Alanube vs The Factory HKA), credential form with provider-specific fields, environment toggle (Sandbox/Production), save button, and test connection - all working correctly.

endpoints_verified: GET /api/system/ecf-credentials/thefactory - Returns The Factory credentials (password masked with ****)
GET /api/system/ecf-credentials/alanube - Returns Alanube credentials (token masked with ****)
PUT /api/system/ecf-credentials - Save The Factory credentials with provider='thefactory'
PUT /api/system/ecf-credentials - Save Alanube credentials with provider='alanube'
PUT /api/system/ecf-credentials - Validates provider must be 'alanube' or 'thefactory' (returns 400 for invalid)
GET /api/ecf/config - Shows both providers status including DB-stored credentials
GET /api/system/config - Masks sensitive e-CF credentials before returning

key_findings.test_connection_result: Conexión exitosa con The Factory HKA

elements_verified: Navigation to Config > Sistema tab - SUCCESS
The Factory HKA provider button (data-testid='ecf-provider-thefactory') - FOUND and SELECTED
TF User field (data-testid='ecf-tf-user') - FOUND, value: xfsbrucwcqtr_tfhka
TF Password field (data-testid='ecf-tf-password') - FOUND, value masked
TF Company field (data-testid='ecf-tf-company') - FOUND, value: PORTERHOUSE SRL
Provider switch to Alanube - Alanube Token and RNC fields appear
Test connection button (data-testid='ecf-test-connection') - SUCCESS toast shown

critical_code_review_comments: EcfCredentialsForm component correctly renders provider-specific fields based on selected provider
Password masking works correctly - values starting with **** are not saved to DB
Environment toggle shows warning for Production mode
DB-first credential resolution implemented correctly in thefactory.py and alanube.py
All data-testid attributes properly implemented for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: e-CF credential management feature fully tested. The EcfCredentialsForm component in SystemTab.js renders when ecf_enabled is true. Provider-specific fields change based on selected provider (Alanube shows Token+RNC, The Factory shows User+Password+RNC+Company). Credentials are stored in system_config collection with keys like ecf_tf_user, ecf_tf_password, ecf_alanube_token. The backend reads DB credentials first, then falls back to .env. Test connection works with DB-stored credentials.
